assessments.status_rationale: Today’s items note major Iran-war escalation and oil prices above $110, but provide no explicit evidence of war-risk insurance quotes for Hormuz transits reaching ≥2.0% of hull value for any voyage.
No news bullet references a U.S. government announcement changing an oil-tanker reinsurance backstop from $20B to ≥$30B.
The provided headlines do not report metrics on Iran’s nationwide internet connectivity returning to ≥70% of baseline for 48 consecutive hours.
No item indicates a recorded committee vote on a bill prohibiting members of Congress/senior officials from profiting from prediction markets.
No evidence in today’s bullets that the CFTC published proposed guidance or an NPRM on political/war-related event contracts.
Today’s news list does not mention any new OFAC cyber-related designations, let alone ≥10 Iran-linked individuals/entities targeting U.S. critical infrastructure.

While the news reflects heightened Iran conflict, none of the bullets show a U.S. public confirmation of deploying an additional THAAD battery or at least two Patriot batteries to the Gulf tied to the escalation.
Headlines indicate oil has surged above $110 and markets are volatile, but they do not provide the quantitative 30-day realized volatility for ICE Brent needed to confirm ≥55% annualized.
No item references OpenAI publishing an updated public policy document on defense/national-security use cases and weaponization constraints.
No item mentions China confirming a successful recovery of an orbital-class reusable rocket first stage.
